pi_items.metadata_value: task.json should have 'metadata' field with key-value pairs
pi_items.verified_value: NO metadata field exists in task.json; only id, version, env_id, description, init, hooks, and success fields present

GCompris is a real, publicly-available educational software suite for children aged 2-10, maintained by KDE (GNU AGPL v3 licensed), with 180+ activities in version 2.3 (installed), and 190+ activities in newer versions (4.0+, 25.x, 26.0 as of Feb 2026)
pi_items.source: Web searches: gcompris.net, github.com/KDE/gcompris, official documentation; apt install log confirms GCompris 2.3-1 installed

pi_items.verified_value: The Maze activity exists in GCompris and is located in the 'Dino/Sports' category in version 2.3
pi_items.source: Evidence documentation (README.md line 36-37): 'Screenshot shows Dino category with tiles: The football game, Maze, Memory game with images...' and ev_t2_maze.png screenshot; web search confirmed maze activity is standard feature

pi_items.verified_value: The maze character is Tux, the Linux penguin mascot. References to 'penguin character' are accurate.
pi_items.source: Web searches mention 'move Tux to the door' and 'Guide Tux out of the maze'; Tux is the official Linux penguin mascot used throughout GCompris

pi_items.verified_value: GCompris Maze activity uses arrow keys or swipe gestures to control the character movement
pi_items.source: Web search results from gcompris.net screenshots confirm 'use arrow keys or swipe the screen to move Tux to the door'

pi_items.verified_value: Lion is one of several possible bonus/completion characters in GCompris (others include flower, gnu, note, smiley, tux)
pi_items.source: Web search result: 'The possible bonus/completion characters in GCompris include flower, gnu, lion, note, smiley, and tux'

pi_items.metadata_value: Dino/Sports category exists and contains Maze
pi_items.verified_value: In GCompris 2.3 (installed), the 'Dino/Sports' category exists with dinosaur icon at coordinates (862,97) in 1920x1080. README confirms it contains Maze tile. However, web searches for GCompris 4.0+ show different category structure (7 main categories: Computer Discovery, Reading, Arithmetic, Science, Geography, Games, Other) with no 'Dino/Sports' category.
pi_items.source: Evidence documentation README.md lines 35-36, 137, 148; apt log confirms GCompris 2.3-1 installed; web searches show newer versions have different categories

pi_items.metadata_value: Modern Qt-based version (comment in install script suggests 180+ activities)
pi_items.verified_value: GCompris version 2.3 is installed (apt log: 'gcompris-qt:amd64 (2.3-1)' installed 2026-02-18). This is NOT the latest version; newer versions exist (4.0, 4.3, 25.0, 25.1.1, 26.0 as of Feb 2026). Version 2.3 is older (likely from 2018-2019 era based on version numbering).
pi_items.source: Install script /workspace/scripts/install_gcompris.sh line 25 installs 'gcompris-qt gcompris-qt-data'; apt log confirms 2.3-1 version; web searches show version progression

pi_items.metadata_value: Comment states 'GCompris 2.3 does NOT support --launch flag'
pi_items.verified_value: Comment in task_utils.sh line 33 is accurate for version 2.3. The setup uses '-m' flag for 'main menu' mode instead. Newer versions may differ.
pi_items.source: task_utils.sh line 33; consistent with installed version 2.3; actual invocation uses -m flag not --launch

pi_items.verified_value: The environment and tasks are based on REAL software (GCompris is a publicly available, open-source project), but the task configuration files, setup scripts, and coordinate mappings are created/configured for this specific benchmark and are thus SYNTHETIC in the sense that they're test configurations, not natural production data.
pi_items.source: GCompris itself is real; task.json, setup_task.sh, coordinates, and environment configuration are created for this benchmark task

privileged_info_summary: GCompris is a real, open-source educational software suite maintained by KDE. The complete_maze task uses GCompris version 2.3 (gcompris-qt:amd64 2.3-1), which features 180+ interactive activities organized into categories including Dino/Sports, Math, ABC/Reading, and Science/Experiment. The Maze activity is confirmed to exist in the Dino/Sports category with a penguin character (Tux, the Linux mascot) that players navigate using arrow keys through a maze to reach a door. Completion shows a bonus animation that can include a lion character among other possible congratulations sprites. All task descriptions in task.json accurately reflect the actual functionality of GCompris 2.3. Note: task.json contains no 'metadata' field; claims verified are from description and related scripts.
